title: Machine Learning, from first principles
subtitle: The ML theory posts, reorganised into reading order
intro: These started as notes written while I worked through the material. Published in the order I wrote them, they interleave several threads at once. Here they are regrouped so each thread runs start to finish.

parts.name: Foundations
parts.blurb: What the subject is, and the mathematics it assumes.

parts.name: The learning problem
parts.blurb: The setup, the objective, and why minimising it on a sample is a leap of faith.

parts.name: Optimization
parts.blurb: How the objective actually gets minimised.

parts.name: Regularization
parts.blurb: Controlling model complexity, and why L1 is sparse.

parts.name: Support vector machines and kernels
parts.blurb: From margins to the dual, and the trick that buys a huge feature space for free.

parts.name: Probabilistic modeling
parts.blurb: Estimating parameters, and the generative view.

parts.name: Bayesian methods
parts.blurb: Treating the parameter as a random variable, and what that buys.

parts.name: Multiclass and structured prediction
parts.blurb: More than two labels, then outputs with internal structure.

parts.name: Trees and ensembles
parts.blurb: The first nonlinear classifier, and why averaging many of them works.

parts.name: Gradient boosting
parts.blurb: Additive modelling, and the generalisation that made boosting a workhorse.
